programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Blonde vs Bear
programme.desc: Documentary following Ann Bryant as she tries to protect bears from tourists in Lake Tahoe. As the summer continues more bears are breaking into houses and Ann battles to save them from certain death, but is it too late for this mother and her cubs?

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: It is feeding time at the sanctuary, and the primate inhabitants must learn the tricky task of finding food for themselves

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: The guys are asked to build a Harry Houdini inspired tank for magicians in Los Angeles. Plus, Wayde King plans out how to celebrate his wedding anniversary to Heather

programme.desc: A cruelty investigator is responding to a call from the sheriff's department. A pony has been dragged down a road, leaving it crippled with pain

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.sub-title: The Dogs Who Stole Our Hearts
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the pit bull shelter, beginning with a look at the dogs that had a major impact on Tia's family and the parolees who helped them

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Kristen, whose cat Moose makes a misery of her life by marking everything in her house. The expert works his techniques to transform its ways

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a woman whose cat has not used a litter box for two years, and another troubled feline terrorises a fellow cat and its owner

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game Wardens Gould and Hickerson chase drug smugglers that crossed the Rio Grande River. Meanwhile, Game Warden Boone checks out an injured kingfisher bird

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Mel and her daughter rescue a baby squirrel. Dr Jeff helps a dog with a serious illness, and then teams up with Dr Petra to save a beloved cat hit by a car

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his team build a butterfly-themed treehouse in a cluster of six trees, featuring a climbing net and a loft, for the residents of a therapeutic centre

programme.title: Gator Boys
programme.desc: Paul and Jimmy find themselves busy capturing alligators on a school playground and in a housing development. Meanwhile, Tre is bitten by a giant Burmese python

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Always pushed to their limits by the elements, circumstances and each other, the Kilchers continue to work together as a family to sustain the rich heritage of homesteading

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Following four groups of ordinary people as they travel into the wilderness of Alaska, where they must find a new home in the inhospitable environment and deal with the threat posed by bears, glaciers and their own fears

programme.title: Alaska Monsters
programme.sub-title: Bigfoot of Cook Inlet: Water Stalker
programme.desc: The native outdoorsmen go after a water-based bigfoot, meaning they must use speedboats, kayaks and water traps in an attempt to capture this elusive creature

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys create their first-ever self-automated homestead in the fierce Alaskan wilderness, while firefighters Kim and Casey Boespflug are away on duty

programme.title: Dog Detectives
programme.desc: Devastating news about PC McIntyre's Alsatian Rudi jeopardises their future together. Meanwhile, sniffer dog Meg has the chance to show her skills on a suspected drug dealer's car

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson shares some of his favourite Treehouse Masters moments, including some previously lost footage

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigator Max Mixson visits a house where more than 100 dogs are reportedly being confined in miserable conditions, but is given an angry reception by the owner

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigator Scott Wernick is unable to save the life of an emaciated horse, but later witnesses the birth of a calf

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy helps a soft-spoken musician who feels abused by her cat and is ashamed she cannot figure out how to prevent her pet's behaviour

programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: Lake Windsor on Inagua Island is home to a mystery. How did giant ocean-dwelling fish get there? The team aims to find out - and catch some bonefish

programme.title: Tanked
programme.sub-title: Learn to Love or Love to Learn
programme.desc: Las Vegas Weddings asks tank manufacturing supremos Brett Raymer and Wayde King to make an old-school Vegas-style tank for their chapel

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Animal control officers have been removing cats from an apartment where a woman has recently been evicted. Some of the animals are in bad shape and need help urgently

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Animal Cruelty Investigators arrive at a property to rescue some horses, but have a surprise in store when they discover a litter of puppies trapped inside a storm drain

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys create their first-ever self-automated homestead in the fierce Alaskan wilderness, while firefighters Kim and Casey Boespflug are away on duty

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees
programme.desc: Former adopters come back to Villalobos to take another dog. Mariah faces a challenging rescue when she realises that the dog in danger is deaf

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a man with Crohn's disease who is worried about his cat's constant moaning, and also diagnoses the problems of an old-age feline

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy looks at a cat who is coming between two lovers. Meanwhile, Jon and Michelle's marriage is riddled with stress over their angry feline Zooey

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: It's the start of hunting season and wardens investigate the illegal shooting of two deer. Then, they swap out bass fish in the Bois d'Arc Lake

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff treats a skittish Savannah cat with an unusual condition. Thee team races to save a stray dog as it runs toward the motorway

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: An arborist pushes Pete Nelson and his team to the limit by requesting a tri-level residence 60 feet above the ground in three giant redwood trees

programme.title: 100 Days Wild
programme.desc: Andrew and Gerrid face a tense race against time to get the moose back to base camp. Meanwhile, the whole community works together to build a goahti

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto pays for his hernia surgery, and develops an eagerness to strike gold. Meanwhile, Atz conquers old fears as he races to complete his cabin before the freeze sets in

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: The harsh conditions begin to take their toll on the volunteers, as they experience a blizzard, freezing rivers and sub-zero temperatures, but know they cannot turn back

programme.title: Alaska Monsters
programme.desc: Documentary following native outdoorsmen as they take on the challenge of exploring the region's treacherous terrain to prove native monsters are linked to disappearances

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil Darish lies low after announcing his plan to develop the motherlode into a mine tour to encourage tourists to visit McCarthy

programme.title: Gator Boys
programme.desc: Jimmy suggests an expansion of the rescue operation, and ends up clashing with Paul. However, when the volunteers begin to take sides in the dispute, the pair must put aside their differences to save their venture from ruin

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson returns to build more spectacular treehouses, beginning with a five-platform structure for a nature preserve in Michigan

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: A litter of puppies faces a struggle for survival, and a malnourished horse has trouble giving birth

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Investigators intervene to save 30 hungry horses in one of the biggest and most challenging rescues ever for the team

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the flamboyant court of French monarch Louis XIV at the Palace of Versailles, where he surrounded himself with aristocrats, artists, interior designers, gardeners, wig-makers, chefs and musicians. Ambitious courtiers scrambled to get closer to the king, but the bedchamber seemed to provide a more unseemly path to power

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter turns her attention to Ruth's trial, which took just a day and a half. She starts with a tape-recorded conversation from the 1980s between Ruth's son Andre and the barrister who led the prosecution. Andre expresses doubts about his mother's trial, calling into question her state of mind and whether she was a cold-blooded killer

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman presents the long-running academic quiz show, in which teams from the University of Sheffield and University College London compete in another first round match

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The Cricket Supporters take on IT Support in the quiz where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. Hosted by Victoria Coren Mitchell

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Show
programme.sub-title: The Morecambe and Wise Show 1968: The Lost Tape
programme.desc: Comedy duo Eric Morecambe and Ernie Wise go a few rounds in the boxing ring and pay a visit to a nudist colony. With music by female singers the Paper Dolls and Greek guitar group Trio Athénée. First broadcast September 16, 1968

programme.title: The Perfect Morecambe and Wise
programme.desc: Another selection of classic sketches and routines from the double act's shows. Eric dons a wig, Tom Jones performs Exactly Like You with the aid of some unlikely backing singers and Judi Dench joins the duo in a parody of the story of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde. Plus, the boys get musical in a performance of There Is Nothing Like a Dame from South Pacific

programme.title: Dame Judi Dench - Five to Eleven
programme.desc: Judi Dench reads The Cultivation of Christmas Trees by TS Eliot and The Cherry Tree Carol Poem by Anon. First broadcast 20 December 1988

programme.title: The Miracle Club (2023)
programme.desc: Chrissie Ahearn arrives in the town of Ballygar from Boston for the funeral of her mother Maureen, 40 years after she was banished by friends and family as a pregnant teenager. Her return coincides with a pilgrimage to Lourdes organised by Father Dermot Byrne. Comedy set in 1968, starring Maggie Smith, Kathy Bates and Laura Linney

programme.title: Personal Cinema: Maggie Smith
programme.desc: In a programme first broadcast in 1970, the actress talks to Michael Aspel about her career and chooses scenes from some of her favourite films, including Way Out West, The Last Hurrah, On the Waterfront, Some Like It Hot and The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Reggae at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of reggae performances from the BBC archives, featuring footage from programmes including Top of the Pops, Old Grey Whistle Test and Later with Jools Holland. Acts include Bob Marley and the Wailers, Gregory Isaacs, Desmond Dekker, Burning Spear, Althea and Donna, Dennis Brown and Buju Banton

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the North European region through its art. He begins by examining the links between landscape, climate and national character, taking in everything from the era of the Vikings to Edvard Munch's The Scream

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 22, 1999. Featuring Macy Gray, B*Witched, ATB, Honeyz, Liam Gallagher and Steve Craddock, Steps, Tina Turner and Christina Aguilera.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 29, 1999. Featuring Tina Turner, Simply Red, Eternal, Will Smith, A.T.F.C. Presents OnePhatDeeva, Destiny's Child and Westlife

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Gary Davies present the May 16 edition, featuring Kim Wilde, Bryan Ferry, Duran Duran, Jimmy Nail, Debarge, Phyllis Nelson, Paul Hardcastle and Divine

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: David Jensen and John Peel present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 10, 1984. Includes appearances from Belle & the Devotions, Queen, the Flying Pickets, Bob Marley & the Wailers, Terri Wells, the Pointer Sisters and Duran Duran

programme.title: Hot Chocolate at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC performances and rare interview extracts celebrating British pop group Hot Chocolate and in particular its lead soloist Errol Brown. The band's top 10 hits from the 1970s and early 1980s are showcased, alongside rarely seen early performances and cult fan favourites

programme.title: When Bob Marley Came to Britain
programme.desc: Documentary examining the reggae superstar's relationship with the UK, and how his universal message of one love and unity helped inspire a generation of young black British people. The film also takes a revealing look at how Marley spent his time while he was in Britain - the houses he lived in, football kickabouts in Battersea Park and visits to the UK's growing Rastafarian community, including secret gigs in the north of England

programme.title: Bob Marley Reimagined
programme.desc: A concert at Birmingham's Town Hall celebrating the reggae superstar, featuring songs including Get Up Stand Up, One Love/People Get Ready, Redemption Song, Satisfy My Soul, Is This Love, I Shot the Sheriff and Exodus. Hosted by Trevor Nelson, the concert features the Chineke! Orchestra joined by Bob's grandson Skip Marley, singer-songwriter JP Cooper and singer Ruby Turner

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A January 1975 edition of the old-time music hall show from the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds, in which chairman Leonard Sachs welcomes performers including Ken Dodd, Barry Kent, Lyn Kennington, John Bouchier and Saxburger to the stage

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 22, 1999. Featuring Macy Gray, B*Witched, ATB, Honeyz, Liam Gallagher and Steve Craddock, Steps, Tina Turner and Christina Aguilera.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on October 29, 1999. Featuring Tina Turner, Simply Red, Eternal, Will Smith, A.T.F.C. Presents OnePhatDeeva, Destiny's Child and Westlife

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Gary Davies present the May 16 edition, featuring Kim Wilde, Bryan Ferry, Duran Duran, Jimmy Nail, Debarge, Phyllis Nelson, Paul Hardcastle and Divine

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: David Jensen and John Peel present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on May 10, 1984. Includes appearances from Belle & the Devotions, Queen, the Flying Pickets, Bob Marley & the Wailers, Terri Wells, the Pointer Sisters and Duran Duran

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Bank Devalued Our House After Approving Our Mortgage
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Louise Minchin and Julia Somerville investigate why a bank suddenly refused to let one couple re-mortgage because of the way their home had been constructed - despite approving the mortgage five years earlier. Plus, a woman being hounded for payments for a phone contract that isn't hers

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is at a mid-terrace house in Stoke-on-Trent that seems too good to be true and Jacqui Joseph is fretting over the short lease on a flat in south-east London. A terraced house in Staffordshire needs a lot more attention than originally thought

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams search for money-making items in Llandeilo, Carmarthenshire, with the help of Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson. Presenter Christina Trevanion gets hands-on with the ancient art of silversmithing at a family business in the nearby town of Laugharne

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Nick Stapleton listen in to calls by a scammer posing as a representative of Sky's broadband department as he tries to steal money from UK victims believing he is fixing their internet connection. But as the team try to contact a woman in the process of being defrauded, they face a previously unseen foe - a fire alarm in the scam hub

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Television and radio presenter Angellica Bell and her husband, former fellow children's TV favourite Michael Underwood, team up with Nicki Chapman to find a family home in the Devon countryside. The property savvy pair show they're still happy to roll up their sleeves and get mucky, as they take on a DIY miniature dream home build, and there are even talks of full-scale renovations at a 1930s period proposal

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having been to the Lake District over 50 times, today's couple have decided to stop visiting and start living in one of the UK's most spectacular national parks. The about-to-be-retired couple need Steve Brown's help to find them, and their two rescue cats, a new forever home in the Cumbrian countryside

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.sub-title: Glasgow - A Fix at the Top of the Cathedral
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Lucia Scalisi help repair Glasgow Cathedral's weathervane at the top of the 225-foot-high spire, save a Chinese unicorn mask made of bamboo and paper that plays and important part in celebrations for Glasgow's Hakka Chinese Community, and discover how street art is changing the city

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Kevin, Dave, Alan and Ken aim to win big by stepping in the right direction, avoiding the wrong answers and making it across the bridge to the other side. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bea is out of options, Mark and Grant's relationship is more strained than ever, while Cindy tries to get through to George

programme.title: Eurovision Song Contest
programme.desc: The second semi-final live from Vienna, Austria, which sees another 15 acts taking the stage in the hope of winning one of the 10 places in Saturday's main show. UK viewers can vote for their favourites, and there's also a chance to see and hear UK act Look Mum No Computer, aka YouTube singer Sam Battle, as he takes to the stage for his first live performance of Eins, Zwei, Drei, which he will be hoping will bag him more than nul points on Saturday

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents topical debate from Brecon, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate how a scammer was able to cancel one man's flight when he believed he was receiving customer support from the airline on social media. Plus, the woman whose cottage holiday didn't live up to expectations - but didn't receive a penny in compensation

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former care centre is converted into a smart family home in Waterlooville, developers sort out a house with subsidence in West Bromwich and a refurbished semi-detached home is given as a gift in Birkenhead

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine is at the helm as the red and blue teams shop for antiques in Herefordshire, with assistance from Richard Madley and Charles Hanson. It all comes down to a battle of the bonus buys at the auction in Wrexham

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Sullivan's dream of leaving Kembleford is scuppered when he is arrested for receiving stolen goods. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: West End and Broadway singer Alfie Boe aims to swap the stage for rural Sussex as Ginny Buckley presents him with a selection of showstopping rural properties. Away from the house-hunting, the duo race agricultural vehicles at a vintage tractor club. Can Alfie find his dream home and keep up with Ginny on the track?

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.sub-title: Midlands - A Celestial Projector Is Brought Back to Life
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Mark Stuckey are in the Black Country, tackling the needs of a big red bus. After being used as a mobile shelter by a homelessness charity for a few years, a leaking roof and outdated electrics have combined to keep it off the road. It's a huge fix to take on, in every sense, and the team needs all the help vintage vehicle restorer Mark Owen can provide if they're to get it back into service. Plus, Pauline presents a celestial projector, once owned by her husband. Now the light has stopped working, and many of the constellations have worn away

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp welcomes a team of drag queens to take part in the game show. The Vivienne, Tia Kofi, Kitty Scott-Claus and Lawrence Chaney walk across the bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, in a bid to take home as much cash as possible for their chosen charity

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: JB Gill and Alex Jones present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Grace Dent present the last quarter-final. The hopefuls begin with an invention test in which they have to serve a sweet or savoury plate of food that makes the most of fruit. Then, restaurant critic Leyla Kazim sets them a brief, asking for a dish that uses an ingredient they used to hate when they were children

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as comedian Roisin Conaty and Karl Turner MP join Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news headlines

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Brendan O'Carroll returns as Agnes Brown, the loudest, proudest woman in Ireland. Without even leaving the house, the doting mother-of-six becomes a podcast sensation, as Cathy is horrified to discover her parent accidentally hijacking her new online venture. Simon Delaney guest stars, with Jennifer Gibney

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Alex is excited when her old mentor pays the ward a visit, but the others are terrified of her. Matt and Serena try to help a patient find out why he keeps suddenly fainting

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.sub-title: I Left a Woman on the Table
programme.desc: The ward is buzzing when Matt's secret wife appears. Bruce and Chaplain Steve compete for Dr Henderson and Joyce puts herself back on the floor as a nurse

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a proud gang of LGBTQ+ friends in Middlesbrough. Dickie, Mam, Lucinda and Sal throw a surprise coming-out party for Stewart at Keith's World of Carpets, which turns into a venture in wonderland. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The queens get Stewart ready for his first date - and decide to invite themselves along as well. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Elijah Young, Mark Benton and Alexandra Mardell

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City. Mark Chapman presents action from the final, as the teams battled it out for the famous trophy at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the final round of fixtures in the Super League, including Liverpool v Arsenal, West Ham United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Manchester United

programme.title: The Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: The work of a team of LGBTQ+ professional party agents as they plan epic events. Best friends Jordan and Tre from London visit the agency. They want to throw an amazing birthday bash for Jordan's boyfriend Corey, but that's only part of the brief - Jordan also wants to spring a surprise proposal on Corey and he's hoping the Big Proud Party Agency can help him with the most important moment of his life

programme.title: The Big Proud Party Agency
programme.desc: The agents are tasked with creating an epic night out for a group of friends soon to head to university

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: One-off comedy about a group of teenagers with varying disabilities on their ride to school on the mobility bus. Mike thinks he is better than his group of 'loser' friends, but it's difficult to break away from them when they all get the bus together. Starring Jack Carroll

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: How animals survive when the northern half of Asia is transformed by snow and ice each year. Featuring footage of Himalayan wolves hunting the fast-moving chiru antelope, red-crowned cranes learning to perform intricate mating dances, and Amur tigers in the forests where Russia, China, and North Korea meet - an area that is also home to the world's largest owls

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: As they reach the halfway point on the tour, some of the group start to struggle and the success of the trip is thrown into question. Freddie prepares the boys for their toughest cricket match so far and smarten them up for a prestigious event with members of Kolkata's high society

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: A face-to-face mission brings out the players' true colours, leading to arguments and new suspicions. At the Circle of Shaq, new players face accusations and the game takes an unexpected turn when the traitors are handed out an explosive secret mission

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: The morning begins with a chilling announcement that leaves the players stunned - someone's fate hangs in the balance after the events of the previous night. As the group retraces their steps, whispers of suspicion and fear ripple through - and the mission takes on an ominous tone as the players work together to uncover who has been murdered

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: As they reach the halfway point on the tour, some of the group start to struggle and the success of the trip is thrown into question. Freddie prepares the boys for their toughest cricket match so far and smarten them up for a prestigious event with members of Kolkata's high society

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: The disappearance of 17-year-old Andie Bell has haunted the town of Little Kilton for five years, a confessional text pointed the blame at her boyfriend Sal after he took his own life. Now, inquisitive teenage amateur sleuth Pip Fitz-Amobi is determined to clear his name and get to the bottom of what really happened. Crime drama based on the novel by Holly Jackson, starring Emma Myers and Zain Iqbal

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Now a team, Pip and Ravi turn their attention to Andie's best friends and find out Andie had a secret life that Sal was unhappy about. Meanwhile, Pip receives an anonymous warning. Crime drama, starring Emma Myers and Zain Iqbal

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Against her better judgement, Pip seeks out a calamity party with hopes of tracking down the drug dealer Max told her about. Ravi is sceptical about the plan but she's determined to see it through with the help of Cara and Lauren

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Dance Race
programme.desc: New Zealand choreographer Corey Baker's short film which a range of dancers, from ballet and Strictly stars to choreographed skydivers and drivers, race across the UK to meet Lady G

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Brazil searching for the jaguar, the world's most elusive big cat. He sees them swimming, meets mothers and cubs, and even witnesses one hunting a caiman

programme.title: Despicable Me 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Dismissed from the Anti-Villain League after botching a mission, Gru sets off to meet his long-lost twin brother for one final criminal mission. Animated sequel, with the voices of Steve Carell, Kristen Wiig, Trey Parker and Miranda Cosgrove

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team find themselves split between Saint Marie and the island of Saint Auguste, attempting to investigate the mysterious death of a British tourist. When the team uncover a series of startling connections between the victim and other guests at the hotel they were staying at, they realise nothing is quite what it seems in this case. Neville prepares to make a huge next step in his life, and a familiar face returns

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a proud gang of LGBTQ+ friends in Middlesbrough. Dickie, Mam, Lucinda and Sal throw a surprise coming-out party for Stewart at Keith's World of Carpets, which turns into a venture in wonderland. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The queens get Stewart ready for his first date - and decide to invite themselves along as well. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Elijah Young, Mark Benton and Alexandra Mardell

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie (2016)
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach the model goes horribly wrong and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and have to flee the country. Comedy based on the BBC sitcom, starring Jennifer Saunders, Joanna Lumley and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a proud gang of LGBTQ+ friends in Middlesbrough. Dickie, Mam, Lucinda and Sal throw a surprise coming-out party for Stewart at Keith's World of Carpets, which turns into a venture in wonderland. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The queens get Stewart ready for his first date - and decide to invite themselves along as well. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Elijah Young, Mark Benton and Alexandra Mardell

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The ultimate hunt for the best British comic begins as 30 hopefuls battle it out across six heats to earn a place in the final, where one will be crowned the BBC New Comedian of 2025. Josh Jones hosts the West heat at St George's, a fully accessible live music and arts venue in the heart of Bristol. Carwyn Blayney, Will Owen, Omari Douglas, Jess Carrivick and Alfie Dundas take to the stage in a bid to impress judges Priya Hall, Harriet Dyer and Fatiha El-Ghorri

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Very average woman Spud cooks, cleans, serves and cares for her father. The only problem is she does it all rather badly - and half-heartedly. A woman who is never seen, unattractive and ignored, she does not feel bad about herself. However, when her father dies, she starts to realise she may have to come out from the shadow and be seen. Comedy drama, written by and starring Siobhán McSweeney

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a proud gang of LGBTQ+ friends in Middlesbrough. Dickie, Mam, Lucinda and Sal throw a surprise coming-out party for Stewart at Keith's World of Carpets, which turns into a venture in wonderland. Written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Mark Benton, Alexandra Mardell and Monica Dolan

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: The queens get Stewart ready for his first date - and decide to invite themselves along as well. Comedy, written by and starring Phil Dunning, with Elijah Young, Mark Benton and Alexandra Mardell

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: A look at how, meticulous and methodical, Big John engineered his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it was flawless, but as the deadline approached, nothing went according to plan. Two battles unfolded simultaneously - a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six more home cooks put themselves in the culinary spotlight in the second heat of the week, ready to show judges Anna Haugh and Grace Dent that they have what it takes. The Signature Dish round sees global influences in abundance, with Polish, Mexican and Brazilian flavours making for an exciting tasting, while Grace and Anna set a daunting Classic Recipe test, which requires the cooks to follow a recipe for an egg yolk and ricotta-filled raviolo, served with a sage and mushroom burnt butter

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Guest judge Patrick Grant joins Diarmuid and Carol as they explore three more contenders for Northern Ireland's Greatest Garden. They visit a coastal sanctuary in Millisle, a 15-acre woodland and wildlife garden in Ballymoney, and a country cottage with herbaceous borders and a cut flower garden in Greyabbey

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Heat three gets underway, which means six new amateur cooks have left their home kitchens ready to compete for one of four coveted MasterChef aprons. The Signature Dish round features a meal they would serve to impress friends or family, before the next challenge requires them to make an authentic salade niçoise. Previous MasterChef winners Jane Devonshire, Dr Saliha Mahmood Ahmed and Thomas Frake assess their efforts

programme.title: LS Lowry: The Unheard Tapes
programme.desc: An insight into the secretive 'matchstick men' painter as he guides viewers through his revealing life story in unheard interviews from the end of his life. Recorded by a young fan, Angela Barratt, in 1972, the tapes uncover much about Lowry's life story and the changing north he captured in his iconic paintings. Lip-synced by actors Ian McKellen and Annabel Smith.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: The quiz heads to Cardiff as Vernon Kay, with Paul Gorton in tow, tests TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz on their UK knowledge

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps mother and daughter Diana and Millie seek out space to bloom in the Somerset countryside and also meets a couple who have planted their own vineyard

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure in Uzbekistan continues in Samarkand, one of the oldest cities in Central Asia. He discovers the national passion for bread, leading him to a family bakery where he learns how to bake special, bagel-like loaves. The great conqueror, Tamerlane, made Samarkand his capital in the 14th-century, and at his tomb Michael learns of the brutality and military prowess of the man who is today Uzbekistan's national hero

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The team visits the RHS Malvern Spring Festival in Worcestershire, where Rachel de Thame and Joe Swift share everything the show has to offer. Gardens, plants and expert advice, as well as tips and tricks to try at home. There is a visit to the Devon-Somerset border to meet a grower who, along with the help of a couple of feathered friends, has transformed a woodland landscape into a stunning nursery. And one designer explains why she is creating her first show to demonstrate the art of pressing flowers

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fourth heat of the classic quiz in which contenders take on the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind Champion. Tonight's specialist subjects are Detectorists, the Alice books of Lewis Carroll, British politics in the 1960s, and Elton John

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team heads to Chepstow to help a multigenerational family of four create a beautiful home, building storage solutions and modernising the family dining room

programme.title: Super Smart Animals
programme.desc: Part one of two. Liz Bonnin sets off on a quest to find the world's most intelligent animals. She has a creative experience with dolphins, meets an octopus escapologist and challenges a chimpanzee to a maths and memory test. Her journey takes her to Los Angeles where she meets Tillman, a skateboarding dog, and John Humphrys joins in by finding out how a goldfish would cope on Mastermind

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team seek out more items of rarity, heading for the gardens of Knightshayes Court in Devon, where they find a copy of a blue-blooded play with royalty on the cast list, a collection of jewellery in a gold-tooled leather box, and a huge pair of rare Doulton vases decorated with kangaroos

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour through Uzbekistan continues in the lush oasis surrounding the historic city of Samarkand. Here, he uncovers the Central Asian origins of the apple, a fruit that travelled to Europe along the Silk Road. Boarding a high-speed train along the old Trans-Caspian Railway, Michael also hears how the Russians toiled to build this remarkable line through the shifting sands of Uzbekistan's steppe

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Emma suspects the pond is being fished by more than their local fishermen and trail cameras capture the visit of a family of hungry otters. The Williamsons take their ram lambs to a prestigious sale on the mainland hoping to make big money and secure their reputation as top tup breeders, while Callum and Zara's children Lucy and Lottie enter the young handlers competition at the Arran agricultural show

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat of this year's competition has arrived, and the final group of six amateurs have been waiting for their shot at culinary stardom. Anna Haugh and Grace Dent will be putting them through their paces across two tasks. The hopefuls include two teachers, a pub manager and a civil servant. The signature dish round kicks off proceedings, with Anna and Grace hoping to sample a creation that represents the amateurs down to a tee

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Porthcawl's crew receive a call about a swimmer who's been swept out to sea in a rip current on the south-west coast of Wales. On the Cornish coast, the Sennen Cove and Penlee crews respond to reports of a 31,000 ton cargo ship that's about to run aground on the Wolf Rock lighthouse. The Chiswick crew race to rescue of a party boat that has lost engine power on the Thames

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Officers are called to a house where a woman has been punched in the face and find themselves confronted by a man with a large knife. There is a tense stand-off before they are able to cuff and arrest him. Dave and Owen come across a group of drug users openly injecting cocaine in broad daylight, while other officers are called to reports of a street fight between two women

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Livestock farmer Joyce has a rare tumour in her bile duct and is undergoing a complex operation to have it removed. During the life-saving procedure, surgeons Anya Adair and Professor Steve Wigmore encounter a difficult dilemma with huge consequences. Also, surgeon Malcolm Will has taken on a rare case involving a tumour that has invaded his patient's chest wall

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London, tackling an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charity's ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers, before Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty flails as Ning continues to push him. Meanwhile, Leanne and Gary bond, until he pressures her to betray Matty to safeguard her family

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps Carol, who wants a home near the sea in south-west Wales for her and her husband. Accompanied by her son Greg, Carol dreams of a picture-perfect double-fronted house with roses around the door. Can Nicki deliver the property she's after?

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour through Uzbekistan continues in the lush oasis surrounding the historic city of Samarkand. Here, he uncovers the Central Asian origins of the apple, a fruit that travelled to Europe along the Silk Road. Boarding a high-speed train along the old Trans-Caspian Railway, Michael also hears how the Russians toiled to build this remarkable line through the shifting sands of Uzbekistan's steppe

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Clive Myrie heads to Kent, where he meets soon-to-be-grandmother Tracey and suggests to her that borrowing a pet might lower her blood pressure. He explains how spending time with animals has been proven to boost happiness, improve wellbeing and lower the risk of dying from cardiovascular disease

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan completes this leg of his journey along Ireland's Wild Atlantic in County Derry/Londonderry. He walks the 400-year-old walls of the city of Derry/Londonderry with comedian Diona Doherty. He also hits the farm to find out what it takes to be a competitive sheep herder, before heading for the windswept coastline of Castlerock to meet a skilled craft worker who builds traditional coracle boats by hand

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the fifth heat of the classic quiz in which contenders take on the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind Champion. Tonight's specialist subjects are Marie Curie, The BBC sitcom Ghosts, Elizabeth Woodville, and Banksy

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: A woman living in a one-bedroom flat in London needs more space, so the team turns her tired and unloved courtyard into an extra living area with some clever storage solutions

programme.title: Super Smart Animals
programme.desc: Part two of two. Liz Bonnin concludes her search for animal intelligence as she encounters grey whales that can display emotions. She also meets Caribbean monkeys able to outwit humans, and communicates with a chimp. The programme also explores the behaviour of Seattle's crows, how bright dogs are capable of learning up to 1,000 words, and why Asian elephants spend hours looking at their reflections in giant mirrors

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and his band of experts value the wares of islanders on Shetland. An enamelled 18th-century glass, a Scottish pearl set in a gold ring and a portrait by celebrated railway artist Terence Cuneo are among the items that catch their eyes

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Tilbury Docks in Essex, antiques experts Elizabeth Talbot and Philip Serrell search for items to take off to auction. Elizabeth finds a mint toy car collection, and Philip unearths a trench periscope from the First World War. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers the explosive history of Britain's biggest gunpowder factory

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Deirdre O'Kane, Jay Rayner, Tessa Sanderson and Iwan Thomas test their general knowledge skills in a final round of challenges before the winner picks up that trophy

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure through Uzbekistan reaches its finale in the historic city of Bukhara. Amidst the medieval Islamic architecture, Michael goes in search of a centuries-old Jewish community. Later he grapples with Kurash, an ancient form of upright wrestling, and his exploration of Uzbekistan culminates in the vast Kyzylkum Desert

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: TV presenter and keen gardener Katie Piper joins Diarmuid and Carol to visit three more gardens. The judges head to Colin's surprising rustic garden in suburban Ballymena, before seeing how Adrienne has transformed two acres near the windswept Glenshane Pass in the foothills of the Sperrin Mountains. Their final stop is in Dromore, Co Down, where Julietta has created a garden designed with her young family in mind, including a wild meadow playground and a courtyard filled with edible plants

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Carole Baxter visits Glenarn, which has a fabulous collection of rhododendrons looking their best. Calum Clunie demonstrates how to take cuttings from a fuchsia, and there are ideas on how to manage a plot if you are unlucky enough to have club root in your vegetable patch

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes a start on planting up some of his spectacular summer containers. He's also got leeks to plant out and shares some advice on how to make your own fertiliser. Frances Tophill heads to Berkshire to learn more about biodynamic gardening and the benefits it can bring. It's also time for an update from Adam Frost's new garden, where things are slowly starting to take shape

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: A look at some of the UK's most beautiful and historic homes, and people who are saving the past for all of us. From a fragile silk robe and a wartime wall painting to a writer's handmade timepiece, the hidden treasures in two houses reveal the stories of two of Britain's most celebrated authors - and the expert teams racing to preserve them. Toby Jones narrates

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee hosts this episode of the comedy showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, featuring stand-up performances by New York-based Edinburgh comedy award winner Alex Edelman, and the Edinburgh Festival Fringe Comedians' Choice Award winner Lou Sanders

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Triangle of Sadness (2022)
programme.desc: Celebrity model couple Carl and Yaya are invited to join a luxury cruise populated by a rogues' gallery of super-rich passengers, including a Russian oligarch and British arms dealers, as well as an idiosyncratic, alcoholic, Marx-quoting captain. But a storm is brewing, and the cruise ends catastrophically. Comedy drama, starring Harris Dickinson, Charlbi Dean and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: Hard-hitting drama written by and starring Richard Gadd - last seen in Netflix thriller Baby Reindeer - and charting the explosive friendship between two men over a 30-year period, since they were brought into each other's lives as youngsters through death and circumstance. In 1993, Niall is conflicted when asked by his mother to lie in court about Ruben's violence. He seems to have changed for the better, so the decision weighs heavily on him. Co-starring Jamie Bell, Marianne McIvor and Neve McIntosh

programme.title: Mastermind: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: The publication of Ann's groundbreaking study, which was followed by a decline in serial crimes. However, she quickly comes to realize her work was not done

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Anna Haugh and Grace Dent present the third quarter-final. The hopefuls begin with an invention test in which they must come up with a brunch dish of their own choosing, making the most of the fresh produce on offer. Then Jimi Famurewa arrives to set them a brief, asking for a dish that uses a nut or nuts of their choice

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay puts the spotlight onto his hometown of Bolton, as TV icon Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and Radio 1Xtra DJ Remi Burgz face a fresh test of knowledge. Also, Paul Gorton delivers the tour, complete with a few extra treats along the way

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: While drilling in the Mesa on Skinwalker Ranch, the team makes a discovery that could literally be out of this world

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Reports of vanishing aircraft and UFOs have convinced investigators that the US government knows more than it admits in the remote deserts of Utah. The top-secret military base Area 52 may hold the answers

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Investigations into the possibility that insects have descended from, and could have links to, extraterrestrials which landed on Earth thousands of years ago

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: According to folklore, pots of gold and wishes are granted if a Leprechaun is caught. These mysterious little creatures are seemingly only found in Ireland. Alleged sightings of leprechauns and fairies are nothing new in Ireland. In fact, many claim they are part of everyday life on the Emerald Isle. Experts debate whether these Celtic creatures are fact or fiction

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: The mysterious phone call from an apparently middle-aged woman who predicted the murder of President John F Kennedy. On November 22 1963, a telephone operator in California answered a call that stated that the President was going to die. This came some hours before Lee Harvey Oswald unleashed his fire

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les goes on the rampage when he finds out a thief has been on the premises, and a customer selling a guitar signed by Kid Rock reveals a valuable secret

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley comes out from behind the security glass to confront a menacing customer, and there is more trouble when a man claims he has been robbed in the shop

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley is in trouble with Seth and Les, who discover she is to blame for the shop's current cash crisis

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: The team heads to Gloucester, where a heart-shaped pendant turns up on Debbie Serpell's table - but she is not sure if she can afford to buy it. Elsewhere, a striking letter seal winds its way to Stewart Hofgartner, and some flying ducks land in the den

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Stamford in Lincolnshire. Michael Hogben is left stunned when a seller questions his generosity, and another fulfils her grandmother's last wishes by coming on the show. Experts Henry Nicholls and Jan Keyne are also in attendance

programme.title: Pawn Stars Do America
programme.desc: Rick Harrison, Corey Harrison and Austin 'Chumlee' Russell end their road trip in the 'Old Man's' home town of Winston-Salem, North Carolina, where they hope to make some sweet Carolina deals. The trio also check out antique tractors, test fire weapons at the range, and hang with a Nascar racing legend

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Cindy and Rick look forward to returning to the auction in Western where they cleaned up last time, while Paul urges Bogart to act on his feelings

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers return to Montebello, California, where Rene and Casey unsuccessfully try to throw off the competition. Ivy secures his skating equipment

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Rene and Casey attempt to shut out Jarrod and Brandi, Darrell uncovers an interesting locker, while Ivy meets a furry friend. Reality series following people who make a living from bidding for the contents of abandoned storage units

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: The bidders return to Dallas hoping to land a locker of a lifetime, and Mary flouts the law in her eagerness to score a dream unit

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas
programme.desc: Auctions in Garland bring a day of dashed hopes and sweet profits. Plus, a new buyer begins to throw some of the bidders off their game

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Witnesses recount seeing unexplained shapes in the deserts of the founder and first khan of the Mongol Empire Genghis Khan. Plus, the mystery fleet of ice planes at the end of the Earth and a look downwards at Kim Jong Un's terror factory from space

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: Egypt's Unexplained Files
programme.desc: An exploration of crime and punishment in ancient Egypt. The topic features homicidal royalty, rampant tomb raiding and acts of extreme punishment

programme.title: Pawn Stars Do America
programme.desc: Rick Harrison, Corey Harrison and Austin 'Chumlee' Russell are ready to mess with Texas, as they take on Austin. With the help of a few trusted companions, they're hoping to round up some gems.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Ivy tries to keep his winning streak going, while Darrell and Rene play mind games with each other. Jarrod and Brandi visit a crime scene and travel back in time

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Brandi is intent on closing the Long Beach store, but Jarrod is determined to save it by scoring more lockers. Ivy tries to intimidate other buyers, while Darrell gets the shock treatment

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Paul tries to persuade his son that they should purchase lockers with an odd number after a lucky poker win, and Ursula buddies up with her pet chinchilla

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Tensions among the buyers are at an all-time high, with the stress of competition on the auction circuit and the buyers facing major decisions with new business ventures

programme.desc: Francesca and Carl race against time to reopen their Italian hamlet for business. Meanwhile, a British-run restored medieval village suffers some damage after a big party, and the owners of a derelict French settlement come to terms with the need to demolish some of its buildings

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Rory Bremner joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner on the words-and-numbers game, chaired by Colin Murray

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Cumbria couple Samantha and Sam want to find their dream holiday home in Spain that works for them and their teenage sons. They know Spain well but have never been to Oliva, so they're placing all their trust in Lee Juggurnauth to find the perfect home at the right place for them. With a budget of £85,000, the pair hop Lee can deliver their Spanish dream

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Attendance at Pastor Simon's church declined during Covid. He's hoping an Easter Sunday sunrise service on his local Atlantic beach will boost his congregation, but it looks like it'll take a miracle to get everything organised in time. Adam and Wayne attempt to put on a live show of their successful Fish'n'Brits podcast to celebrate its first year on air. Laura and Lindsay celebrate the arrival of their son Elliott with a family outing to the Universal theme park where they were married 10 years ago

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Jane and Rory's handmade kitchen island won't fit through the gîte door at Château de Pican. Bart and Sarah rush to prepare their Cognac barn for their best friend's wedding, and at Château de la Vergne, Lionel and Claire work on the gilded ceiling for their dining room and bespoke fromage cupboard

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, Rodriguez dream team Charlotte and brother-in-law Damon search for a property within walking distance of a beach for Northamptonshire couple Steve and Amanda. In Murcia, Natalie Brown and sales manager Charlie are on the hunt for a family friendly holiday home for property developer Steve and his partner Hannah, fun-loving workaholics from Yorkshire

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: The funnyman rides the rails of eastern Germany, where he finds himself in the cab of an unusual electric coal-transporting train and marvels at the ingenuity of the prisoners of Colditz. From there he takes a 'car on rails' made from a Trabant, dives underground into a silver mine by electric train, drives a huge 19th-century locomotive - his biggest steam train yet - and drives a modern tram in Dresden while taking in the rebuilt city's architecture

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Amy Gledhill gets creative with a series of spoons. Kumail Nanjiani impresses everyone with his extraordinarily strong jaw and Alex Horne causes Joanna Page to scream in such terror that it sparks alarm in the local area. Armando Iannucci douses a coconut in vinegar, while Joel Dommett slathers another in glue. Greg Davies hands out the points

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang compete against Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. Regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley and lexicographer Susie Dent are on hand to adjudicate, while David O'Doherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: TFI Friday Unplugged
programme.desc: Chris Evans presents more chat and music, featuring Bob Odenkirk, Danny Dyer, Emilia Clarke, Nigel Ng, Tom Ogden and Joe Donovan from Blossoms, and Gary Lightbody

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The participants are encouraged to explore their sexual fantasies, from submission and dominance to bondage and impact play. programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The group explore how basic skills can transform intimacy. In the workshop, they practise kissing, which is exciting for some but daunting for others. Alex and Joy aim to finally conquer their individual issues as their relationship grows closer, while Shelby unlocks Millie's buried emotions and desires

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his visit to Zayna Flaming Grill in Redondo Beach, California, and finds more questions than answers as he tries to save the crisis-ridden restaurant from disaster. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from south-west London compete for the £1,000 prize, beginning with butchers Roland and Anne. programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.sub-title: Horse Box, Milk Float and Balancing Barn

programme.desc: Ray and the family attend an open day at the twins' school, during which first-grader Michael shocks and embarrasses them by reading out a story obviously based on their constant bickering

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank becomes jealous when Marco begins spending time with Marie, and insists they end their friendship. His actions get the backing of Ray, who does not approve of married women having male pals - especially not his mother. Starring Ray Romano and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray loses his wedding ring and is propositioned by an attractive woman at the airport, so a jealous Debra decides to get her own back - with disastrous results. Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton star

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is up for a prestigious title at his wine club and offers to host the next tasting in his apartment. programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. A miserly pay rise for staff prompts Frasier to cross swords with station manager Kate, but a meeting between the two sees them engage in more than negotiations as emotions are put to the test. Comedy, guest starring Mercedes Ruehl

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: The Adventures of Bad Boy and Dirty Girl
programme.desc: Part two of two. Comedy, guest starring Mercedes Ruehl, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: The Last Time I Saw Maris
programme.desc: Niles reaches the end of his tether with Maris, and decides to retaliate - but she turns the tables by throwing him out and asking for a divorce. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Lido, Anna Vincenzo's and Le Bistro
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, returning to Lido, Anna Vincenzo's and Le Bistro to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Glastonbury in Somerset is today's location, where Henry Cole fashions a quirky lamp out of traffic lights and Simon O'Brien makes a Flintstones-style table out of old roof tiles

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: Anneli, the manager of a restored medieval village in France, is having a tricky time setting up the last wedding party of the season. Elsewhere, the owners of a French hamlet celebrate their first restored building. Finally, passionate estate agent Mark spends his life hunting derelict villages in Galicia

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Rory Bremner joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps a couple from Derbyshire to find a holiday home on Spain's Orihuela Costa, with at least three bedrooms to accommodate all the family, plus a communal pool

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Sidhbh and Neasa launch their new Miami clinic with a party in an operating theatre and a signature cocktail. They hope other local plastic surgeons will book the space for their own operations to help pay off some of its multi-million-dollar cost. Tony and Keegan head to Florida's original theme park to film a review for their YouTube channel and try out some new filming techniques

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul need a winch to complete their huge balcony installation in Brittany. Jane and Rory have a smashing time in their gîte kitchen at Château de Pican, and near Cognac, Sarah and George use their antiques expertise for another wedding

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Steve and Shaun Garner are house-hunting with fellow Brummies Janice and Stephen, who after long careers in healthcare deserve a permanent break from work in sun-soaked Almería

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Sahar Zand reports from the Philippines on the controversial world of spiritual healers. Across the country, with high medical costs and difficulties accessing healthcare, patients put their trust in alternative treatments to cure anything from chronic pain to cancer. Faith healers, blending Catholic rituals with centuries-old traditions, promise miraculous recoveries through prayer, herbal remedies and psychic surgery. Their clinics attract patients from across this country of 112 million people, and even overseas. Sahar meets families placing their hopes in these treatments, including those who've turned away from conventional medicine. Some say they've witnessed extraordinary recoveries, crediting healers with saving their lives. On the outskirts of Manilla, three faith healers who claim to treat over 100 people per week believe they've been divinely chosen to cure conditions modern medicine can't detect. But critics warn that the industry is dangerously unregulated. Medical professionals fear that patients are being misled, delaying vital treatment, often fatally. She hears from people who believe that their loved ones paid the ultimate price for putting their trust in miracle cures that they now believe were a scam. With faith healers operating across the country, authorities face mounting pressure to act, but regulation remains limited in a country where faith runs deep

programme.title: Perfect Pub Walks
programme.desc: Comedian Chris McCausland joins Alexander on three monumental Monmouthshire pub walks through the Wye Valley where they explore castles and ruins, picnic like a Georgian gentleman and climb a holy mountain. As they make their way from pub to pub, Chris tells Xander his remarkable life story and shares emotional insights into his private life, as he discusses his blindness, his family, his career and the other people's expectations of him

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Social activist Aaron is paired with youth worker and nightclub host Nathan - and while they might seem like strangers, their date leads to a twist neither of them is expecting. Former mayor Tracey meets toilet roll salesman Stuart, while barber Verity is paired with 6ft 5 Mancunian coach driver Liam. Burlesque dancer Double D, aka Deborah, is ready to steal the show, and maybe someone's heart. Her date Lee seems like the perfect match - a professional singer who is hoping to turn his solo act into a romantic duet

programme.title: The Last Duel (2021)
programme.desc: In medieval France, squire Jacques Le Gris secures a captaincy destined for knight Jean de Carrouges. To rub salt into fresh wounds, Jacques' promotion includes land intended for his rival. Jean canters off to war and returns to his wife Marguerite, who claims she was sexually assaulted by Jacques. Ridley Scott's epic drama based on a book by Eric Jager, starring Adam Driver, Jodie Comer, and Matt Damon and Ben Affleck (who also worked on the screenplay)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Amy Gledhill gets creative with a series of spoons. Kumail Nanjiani impresses everyone with his extraordinarily strong jaw and Alex Horne causes Joanna Page to scream in such terror that it sparks alarm in the local area. Armando Iannucci douses a coconut in vinegar, while Joel Dommett slathers another in glue. Greg Davies hands out the points

programme.title: How to Build a Girl (2019)
programme.desc: A 16-year-old living in Wolverhampton finds comfort in placing portraits of her media idols covering her bedroom wall. She dreams of escaping her humdrum circumstances by becoming a writer, and manages to secure a trial as a music journalist. Eager to reinvent herself, she adopts a new style, bright red hair and an outlandish pen name. In due course she realises that cruel reviews offer a pathway to success allowing her to support her family financially. Drama based on Caitlin Moran's memoir, with Beanie Feldstein, Cleo and Donal Finn

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Redondo Beach, California, for the first of two visits to Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece Faye and Brenda. Faye believes she has the weight of the entire restaurant on her shoulders, while Brenda feels she isn't treated as an equal in the business. With tensions rising, the food not up to scratch and everyone playing the blame game, the chef is faced with one of the trickiest situations he's ever encountered

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The participants are encouraged to explore their sexual fantasies, from submission and dominance to bondage and impact play. Millie, Ed and Will begin to discover new desires and confidence, while others, like Bertie, question whether this part of the programme is suited to them

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: Plan B (2021)
programme.desc: After a regrettable first sexual encounter, a straight-laced high school student and her slacker best friend have 24 hours to hunt down a morning after pill in America's heartland. Comedy, starring Kuhoo Verma and Victoria Moroles

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.desc: Frank offers to paint Ray's house - but refuses to use the colours he and Debra wanted. Ray Romano and Peter Boyle star

programme.desc: Niles suggests he and Frasier set up practice together - but after a series of disasters, they end up in need of therapy themselves. Comedy, guest starring Milo O'Shea, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce
